2604.27092.title: End-to-end autonomous scientific discovery on a real optical platform
2604.27092.paper_summary: Scientific research has long been human-led, driving new knowledge and transformative technologies through the continual revision of questions, methods and claims as evidence accumulates. Although large language model (LLM)-based agents are beginning to move beyond assisting predefined research workflows, none has yet demonstrated end-to-end autonomous discovery in a real physical system that produces a nontrivial result supported by experimental evidence. Here we introduce Qiushi Discovery Engine, an LLM-based agentic system for end-to-end autonomous scientific discovery on a real optical platform. Qiushi Engine combines nonlinear research phases, Meta-Trace memory and a dual-layer architecture to maintain adaptive and stable research trajectories across long-horizon investigations involving thousands of LLM-mediated reasoning, measurement and revision actions. It autonomously reproduces a published transmission-matrix experiment on a non-original platform and converts an abstract coherence-order theory into experimental observables, providing, to our knowledge, the first observation of this class of coherence-order structure. More importantly, in an open-ended study involving 145.9 million tokens, 3,242 LLM calls, 1,242 tool calls, 163 research notes and 44 scripts, Qiushi Engine proposes and experimentally validates optical bilinear interaction, a physical mechanism structurally analogous to a core operation in Transformer attention. This AI-discovered mechanism suggests a route towards high-speed, energy-efficient optical hardware for pairwise computation. To our knowledge, this is the first demonstration of an AI agentic system autonomously identifying and experimentally validating a nontrivial, previously unreported physical mechanism, marking a milestone for research-level autonomous agents.
2604.27092.paper_summary_zh: 科學研究長期以來一直由人類主導，透過不斷修訂問題、方法和主張來推動新知識和變革性技術，隨著證據的積累而進行。儘管基於大型語言模型（LLM）的代理開始超越協助預定的研究工作流程，但尚未有任何代理在實際物理系統中展示出端到端的自主發現，並產生有實驗證據支持的非平凡結果。在此，我們介紹了 Qiushi Discovery Engine，一個基於 LLM 的自主科學發現系統，能在真實的光學平台上進行端到端的自主科學發現。Qiushi Engine 結合了非線性研究階段、Meta-Trace 記憶和雙層架構，以維持在涉及數千次 LLM 媒介推理、測量和修訂行動的長期調查中的適應性和穩定研究軌跡。它自主地重現了一個在非原始平台上的已發表傳輸矩陣實驗，並將一個抽象的相干序理論轉化為實驗可觀測量，據我們所知，這是首次觀察到這類相干序結構。更重要的是，在一項涉及 1.459 億個標記、3,242 次 LLM 調用、1,242 次工具調用、163 條研究筆記和 44 個腳本的開放式研究中，Qiushi Engine 提出了並實驗驗證了光學雙線性相互作用，這是一種在結構上類似於 Transformer 注意力中的核心操作的物理機制。這一 AI 發現的機制暗示了朝向高速度、節能的光學硬體進行成對計算的路徑。據我們所知，這是首次展示 AI 代理系統自主識別並實驗驗證一個非平凡的、先前未報告的物理機制，標誌著研究級自主代理的一個里程碑。
2604.27092.author: Shuxing Yang et.al.
2604.27092.authors: Shuxing Yang, Fujia Chen, Rui Zhao, Junyao Wu, Yize Wang, Haiyao Luo, Ning Han, Qiaolu Chen, Yuze Hu, Wenhao Li, Mingzhu Li, Hongsheng Chen, Yihao Yang
